questions.scenario: A hull breach in the lower decks threatens thirty sleeping colonists. Do you deploy a dangerous, untested patch that could stabilize the deck but risks an immediate ship-wide decompression (Ingenuity), or do you join your crew in the breach to manually hold the seals, promising to share their fate if the vacuum takes them (Solidarity)?

To ensure a peaceful future for all Nomads and avoid a civil war, you have the chance to falsely testify against a popular but corrupt leader. Do you prioritize the long-term survival of the fleet (Vision) or refuse to bear false witness, even if it means the tyrant stays in power (Integrity)?

An ancient alien artifact is emitting a unique signal that could revolutionize Nomad travel, but it causes agonizing physical pain to a nearby colony. Do you keep it active to harvest its data for science (Curiosity) or disable it immediately to grant the colonists peace (Empathy)?

After a solar flare fries the hydroponics, rations are at 5%. Do you personally endure total starvation to show the fleet that a Nomad can survive on spirit alone (Resilience), or do you enforce a mathematical division of every scrap so the elderly and weak receive the same as the strong (Equity)?

You discover a drifting warp-drive from a lost, advanced civilization. Do you strip its exotic components to fix your own ship's failing life support (Ingenuity), or leave it intact, risking your own systems to spend months deciphering its origins and purpose (Curiosity)?

The fleet is paralyzed by a choice of paths. Do you stay with your childhood friends in a dying, resource-poor nebula because of a sacred oath to never leave them (Solidarity), or lead a splinter group toward a fertile star system to ensure the species does not go extinct (Vision)?

A desperate parent has stolen medicine to save their dying child, a crime that traditionally demands exile into the void. Do you uphold the harsh law of the stars to maintain order (Integrity), or grant them mercy and find another way to balance the scales (Empathy)?

While trapped in a deep gravity well with failing engines, do you push your physical body to the point of permanent injury to manually crank the landing gear (Resilience), or spend your final minutes of oxygen trying to rig a clever remote-control bypass (Ingenuity)?

A shipment of rare luxury spices is recovered. Do you distribute small portions to every person who hasn't tasted home in years (Equity), or give the entire supply to the combat pilots who just saved the ship to bolster their morale (Solidarity)?

With only one deep-space probe remaining, do you launch it into the event horizon of a black hole to solve a fundamental cosmic mystery (Curiosity), or toward a distant, hazy planet that shows the first signs of being a permanent home for your people (Vision)?

During a brutal interrogation by Kilrathi captors, do you remain silent to maintain your honor and the sanctity of your secrets (Integrity), or do you focus on surviving at any cost, weaving a web of distracting lies to keep yourself alive (Resilience)?

Two patients require a life-saving drug, but the Puck's med-bay only has one dose. One is a brilliant, aging doctor; the other is a lonely orphan. Do you give it to the child out of mercy (Empathy), or use a lottery system to ensure the choice is governed by total fairness (Equity)?

Your captain has committed a minor administrative crime to bypass Elders' red tape and feed the crew. Do you stand by your commanding officer to preserve unit cohesion (Solidarity), or report the infraction to the Elders to maintain the purity of Nomad law (Integrity)?

In the ship's workshop, do you devote your talent to creating a clever, temporary tool to solve today's mechanical failures (Ingenuity), or spend your years drafting the architectural blueprints for a massive ship you will never live to see built (Vision)?

Strange radiation is leaking into your quarters. Do you remain inside to record the specific effects on your own biology for the advancement of science (Curiosity), or seal the leak and endure the physical exhaustion of a grueling, immediate repair (Resilience)?

As the ship's main reactor flickers, do you enforce a strict, equal blackout that plunges everyone into darkness (Equity), or allow the engineering deck to keep their power so they can try to build a more efficient generator (Ingenuity)?

A slow-moving colony ship full of civilians is hindering your escape from a nearby supernova. Do you slow your engines to shield them from the blast (Empathy), or leave them behind to ensure the fleet’s most talented 'Vanguards' survive (Vision)?

The crew is weary and begs to return to the main fleet, but a nearby unstable nebula holds the secret to eternal energy. Do you follow the wishes of your people (Solidarity) or convince them to risk everything by diving into the unknown for knowledge (Curiosity)?

You gave your personal word to a friend that they could have the first pick of salvage, but a group of refugees has a much more desperate need. Do you keep your sacred promise (Integrity) or break it to ensure a fair distribution to the needy (Equity)?

Do you choose to settle on a harsh, barren moon to prove the strength and endurance of the Nomad spirit (Resilience), or spend your life in transit, searching for a garden world that may only exist in ancient legends (Vision)?

A malfunctioning shipboard AI is crying out in digital agony. Do you dismantle its core to use its processors for a much-needed navigation boost (Ingenuity), or spend hours providing comfort while its consciousness slowly fades (Empathy)?

You discover a forbidden archive from the early days of the Exodus. To access its secrets, you must break a sacred Nomad oath. Do you seek the hidden history of your people (Curiosity), or maintain the integrity of your vow (Integrity)?

A massive, vital component must be carried across a toxic wasteland. Do you share the weight with others to build a bond through shared struggle (Solidarity), or carry the entire burden yourself to spare your comrades the pain (Resilience)?

To build a grand, multi-generational library for the future, you must heavily tax the poorest Nomads today. Do you build the monument to progress (Vision), or cancel the project to keep their meager lives fair (Equity)?

You can win a prestigious star-race by 'tuning' your engines past safety limits and lying to the inspectors. Do you win through clever deception (Ingenuity), or race with an honest machine and accept a certain loss (Integrity)?

A comrade is collapsing during a forced march through a desert. Do you stop to hold their hand and offer comfort in their final moments (Empathy), or keep moving to demonstrate the cold strength required for the group to survive (Resilience)?

The fleet's remaining credits can fund either a modest school available to all children (Equity) or a single, high-tech observatory to watch the movements of the stars (Curiosity). Which do you authorize?

A new alliance with an alien race offers a prosperous future but requires you to abandon ancient, traditional customs. Do you evolve for the sake of the future (Vision) or refuse to change the ways that bind your people (Solidarity)?
